response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer but has no demonstrable experience in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, deep learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems. The role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on silicon-level performance for AI accelerators, which is a fundamentally different discipline from the candidate's web/backend/full-stack background. No PhD or Master's, and the bachelor's is in CS but without the specialized hardware/architecture experience required at principal level.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) of senior software engineering across diverse stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang microservices handling millions of req/sec at Sociomantic
Brief C/C++ work on LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments touches engineering-tooling space
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerators, or hardware-software co-design
No background in deep learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD
Profile is full-stack/web product engineering, not silicon performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload optimization or GPU/SIMD background
Senior IC tenure and systems-programming exposure
